headline: PDPA Reform Stalls Despite 32 Million Records Exposed
context: Reform of Personal Data Protection Act stalled despite 12 major breaches affecting 32 million citizen records since 2022.

cards.big: PDPA reform stalls despite 12 breaches exposing 32 million records
cards.sub: Malaysia 2010 data law lacks mandatory breach notification requirements

cards.big: Largest breach exposed 18 million from telecom company in 2024
cards.sub: Company faced only RM300,000 fine under current maximum penalty

cards.big: Proposed amendment increases maximum from RM500,000 to RM10 million
cards.sub: Industry lobbying delayed bill through 3 parliamentary sessions since 2023

cards.big: Malaysia ranks 45th globally in data protection behind Thailand and Philippines
cards.sub: Both neighbours updated frameworks in 2024 with stronger provisions

cards.big: PDPA delay tests whether industry lobbying can indefinitely block citizen data protection
32 million records exposed while PDPA reform stalls. Data protection delayed is data protection denied.
